message:
refactor: centralize asupersync re-exports behind fcp-async-core facade

Downstream crates were importing asupersync types (HTTP client, TLS,
WebSocket, RaptorQ, Cx, ReadBuf) directly, coupling them to the raw
runtime's module layout.

- Add pub mod http, tls, websocket, and raptorq to fcp-async-core
  re-exporting the relevant asupersync types under stable names.
- Make compatibility_cx() and the Cx type public so every crate that
  needs an async context goes through one canonical helper.
- Migrate all downstream crates (fcp-graphql, fcp-oauth, fcp-raptorq,
  fcp-streaming, fcp-tailscale, fcp-telemetry) to import exclusively
  through fcp-async-core, eliminating direct asupersync source deps.
- Add compile-time reexport resolution test to fcp-async-core.

No behavioral changes; only import paths are affected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
